analysis: Gate 1B — post-gate robustness of the effective-kernel mechanism
status: prespecified grid frozen before execution; NOT preregistered, NOT confirmatory
relationship_to_gate1: Post-gate robustness analysis. Does not alter, overwrite, or retrospectively reinterpret the frozen confirmatory Gate 1 (config/effective_kernel_gate1_frozen.json). Gate 1 remains canonical: K=15, r=0.7, past_mass=0.3, seed=1, u0=0, epsilon=1e-4.
operating_point_convention: u0 is the raw (pre-squash, z-scored) constant input. The frozen channel applies logistic squashing, so u0 in {-0.5,0.0,0.5} maps to encoded amplitudes {0.37754,0.5,0.62246}, all interior to (0,1) with no clipping; u0=0 reproduces the Gate 1 operating point exactly.

classification.ROBUST_WITHIN_GRID: >=90% of valid configurations pass all four tangent tolerances simultaneously AND <=10% pass all four separable tolerances simultaneously
classification.MIXED: outside the ROBUST bounds, but tangent passes all four simultaneously in >50% of valid configurations and clearly exceeds the separable hypothesis
classification.NOT_ROBUST_WITHIN_GRID: tangent passes all four simultaneously in <=50% of valid configurations, OR the separable construction passes all four simultaneously in >=50%
classification.INVALID: incomplete grid, non-finite unclassified artifacts, invalid provenance, or integrity failure

valid_definition: A configuration is valid iff its fixed point converged AND no exception occurred AND all four confirmatory response arrays are finite. Locally-unstable-but-finite configurations remain valid and are additionally reported over the stable-only subset.
